highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.111: Opus 4.7 xhigh Effort Level & Auto Mode for Max
highlights.summary: Claude Opus 4.7 now supports a new 'xhigh' effort level, sitting between 'high' and 'max', giving you finer control over the speed vs. intelligence tradeoff. You can activate it via the /effort command, --effort flag, or the model settings. Max subscribers also gain access to auto mode when using Opus 4.7.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.113: Native Binary, Network Domain Blocking
highlights.summary: Claude Code now spawns a native binary per platform instead of bundled JavaScript, which should improve startup performance and reliability. A new sandbox.network.deniedDomains setting lets you block specific domains even when a broader wildcard in allowedDomains would otherwise permit them, giving you tighter network security control.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.110: /tui Fullscreen Mode & /focus Command
highlights.summary: A new /tui fullscreen command switches Claude Code to flicker-free rendering within your current conversation, improving visual stability during long sessions. The Ctrl+O shortcut now toggles verbose transcript only, while the new /focus command handles the focus view toggle separately.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest model available in Claude Code, featuring enhanced reasoning capabilities and the new xhigh effort tier. This is a significant upgrade for developers who rely on Claude Code for complex, multi-step coding tasks.

highlights.title: Claude Code Routines Documentation
highlights.summary: The official Claude Code docs now cover Routines — reusable sequences of instructions you can define to automate repetitive workflows inside Claude Code. This is a powerful feature for intermediate users who want to standardize common tasks like project scaffolding or code review steps.

highlights.title: FULL Claude Code Tutorial for Beginners in 2026! (Step-By-Step)
highlights.summary: A comprehensive step-by-step video tutorial covering how to get started with Claude Code in 2026, ideal for developers who are brand new to the tool. It walks through setup, basic commands, and practical usage patterns to help you become productive quickly.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This video aims to bring you from beginner to confident Claude Code user in just 30 minutes, making it one of the most time-efficient tutorials available. It is a great option for developers who already have some AI coding experience and want a fast, focused overview of Claude Code's most important features.

highlights.title: Claude Code OAuth Down for >12 Hours (HN Discussion)
highlights.summary: A Hacker News thread highlights a widespread OAuth authentication issue in Claude Code v2.1.107 where users were stuck on the 'Paste code here if prompted' step with no way to proceed. If you hit this issue, updating to v2.1.113 or later is the recommended fix, as recent releases have addressed authentication stability.

tips_of_the_day: Use /effort xhigh when tackling complex refactors or architecture decisions — it gives you more intelligence than 'high' without the full cost of 'max', striking a practical balance for most advanced tasks.

Define a Claude Code Routine for your most repeated workflows (e.g., 'create a new React component with tests') so you can trigger multi-step tasks with a single command instead of re-typing instructions every session.

If you need tighter security in sandboxed environments, pair sandbox.network.allowedDomains wildcards with sandbox.network.deniedDomains to explicitly block known risky subdomains, giving you allowlist convenience with denylist precision.
